pull.title: refactor: remove ret from secp256k1_ec_pubkey_serialize
pull.body:
This is a follow-up to https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/pull/1774#discussion_r2539737079

It is pretty straightforward to remove `ret` and to just return either `0` or `1`

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

events.body:
ACK 3daab83a6050805763da5ac9ebe8217d1965eb0c

(fwiw https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/pull/1774#discussion_r2539737079 suggested to return early if `_pubkey_load` fails, reducing the levels of indentations, but either way seems to be fine, and I haven't seen a strong preference for one approach over another in our codebase)
